User.description: Represents a user in the EasyKonnect platform. Users can be SERVICE_USER (customers) or SERVICE_PROVIDER (vendors).

AdminUser.description: Admin user (ADMIN or SUPER_ADMIN). Separate auth system from regular users.

Service.description: A service offering by a provider.

Booking.description: A service booking made by a user. Contains service details, scheduling info, pricing, and status tracking through the booking lifecycle (PENDING → ACCEPTED → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED).

Payment.description: Payment record for a booking. Tracks payment status, amount, commission, provider payout, and transaction details.

Review.description: Customer review for a completed booking. Contains rating (1-5) and optional comment.

BookingStats.description: Booking statistics summary. For users: totalBookings, pendingBookings, completedBookings, cancelledBookings, totalSpent. For providers: same plus totalRevenue and completionRate.

PaginatedBookings.description: Paginated booking list with items array and pagination metadata.

AuthResponse.description: Response after successful user/provider login with JWT tokens.

RegistrationResponse.description: Response after registration indicating verification required.

VerifyEmailResponse.description: Response after email verification with auto-login tokens.

register.description:
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
👤 NORMAL USER MUTATIONS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
APIs for regular users (SERVICE_USER) to manage their accounts. These endpoints handle user registration, authentication, password management, and profile updates.
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📋 REGISTER NEW USER ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Creates a new SERVICE_USER account in the EasyKonnect platform. Upon successful registration, a 6-digit OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to the provided email address for verification. The user CANNOT log in until their email is verified.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User signup/registration page
• First step in the onboarding flow
• Creating new customer accounts

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates email uniqueness and password strength
2. Creates user account with PENDING status
3. Sends verification OTP to email via Mailtrap
4. Returns success message (NOT tokens - user must verify first)

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: Public (anyone can register)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Password must be 8+ characters with uppercase, lowercase, and number
• Email must be unique across the platform
• OTP expires in 10 minutes
• User status is PENDING until email verification

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show loading state while API processes
• On success, redirect to OTP verification screen
• Store email in state/localStorage for verification step
• Display password strength indicator

input.description: User registration details including email, password, and personal information

verifyEmail.description:
📋 VERIFY EMAIL ADDRESS WITH OTP

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Verifies the user's email address using the 6-digit OTP sent during registration. On successful verification, the user's status changes from PENDING to ACTIVE, and authentication tokens are returned - effectively logging the user in automatically.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• After user registration (signup step 2)
• OTP verification screen
• Email confirmation flow

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates the OTP against stored value
2. Updates user status to ACTIVE
3. Sets isEmailVerified to true
4. Generates and returns JWT tokens (accessToken + refreshToken)
5. Returns user profile data

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: Public (unverified users)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• OTP expires after 10 minutes
• Maximum 3 failed attempts before requiring new OTP
• Returns tokens on success - no need for separate login

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Provide 6-digit OTP input field
• Auto-submit when 6 digits entered
• On success, store tokens securely and redirect to dashboard
• Show 'Resend OTP' button for expired codes

input.description: Email address and 6-digit OTP code received via email

resendVerificationOtp.description:
📋 RESEND VERIFICATION OTP

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Requests a new verification OTP to be sent to the user's email. Use this when the original OTP has expired (after 10 minutes) or wasn't received. The previous OTP is invalidated when a new one is sent.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User didn't receive the OTP email
• OTP has expired (10 minute limit)
• User needs to re-verify email

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Invalidates any existing OTP for this email
2. Generates new 6-digit OTP
3. Sends new OTP via email
4. Resets expiration timer (10 minutes)

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: Public

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Rate limited to prevent abuse (max 3 per hour)
• Only works for unverified accounts
• Previous OTP becomes invalid immediately

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Add 60-second cooldown timer before allowing resend
• Show 'Check spam folder' reminder
• Disable button during cooldown

input.description: Email address to resend verification OTP to

login.description:
📋 USER LOGIN / AUTHENTICATION

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Authenticates a user with email and password credentials. Returns JWT tokens (accessToken and refreshToken) along with the complete user profile. Works for both SERVICE_USER and SERVICE_PROVIDER accounts.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Login page/screen
• Re-authentication after session expiry
• Switching accounts

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates email/password combination
2. Checks account status (must be ACTIVE)
3. Verifies email is confirmed
4. Updates lastLoginAt timestamp
5. Generates JWT tokens
6. Returns user data with provider profile (if applicable)

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER, SERVICE_PROVIDER

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Account locks after 5 failed attempts (30 minute lockout)
• Email must be verified before login is allowed
• Access token expires in 15 minutes
• Refresh token expires in 30 days
• SUSPENDED accounts cannot login

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Store accessToken in memory (not localStorage for security)
• Store refreshToken securely (httpOnly cookie preferred)
• Check user.role to redirect to appropriate dashboard
• Implement 'Remember me' with refresh token persistence

refreshToken.description:
📋 REFRESH ACCESS TOKEN

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Generates a new access token using a valid refresh token. Use this when the access token has expired but the user's session should continue. This enables seamless user experience without requiring re-login.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Access token expired (401 response)
• Proactive token refresh before expiry
• Silent authentication on app startup

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates the refresh token
2. Checks user account is still active
3. Generates new access token
4. Returns new token with current user data

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required (uses refresh token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: Any authenticated user

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Refresh token expires in 30 days
• If refresh fails, user must login again
• Original refresh token remains valid

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Implement axios/fetch interceptor for automatic refresh
• Queue failed requests and retry after refresh
• Redirect to login if refresh fails
Valid refresh token obtained from login or verifyEmail

forgotPassword.description:
📋 REQUEST PASSWORD RESET

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Initiates the password reset flow by sending a 6-digit OTP to the user's email. For security reasons, this endpoint always returns success even if the email doesn't exist in the system (prevents email enumeration attacks).

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• 'Forgot Password' link on login page
• Password recovery flow - Step 1
• User cannot remember their password

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Looks up user by email (silently fails if not found)
2. Generates 6-digit reset OTP
3. Sends OTP to email
4. OTP valid for 10 minutes

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: Public

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Always returns success (security measure)
• OTP expires in 10 minutes
• Rate limited to prevent abuse

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show 'Check your email' message regardless of response
• Redirect to OTP entry screen
• Don't confirm if email exists

resetPassword.description:
📋 RESET PASSWORD WITH OTP

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Completes the password reset flow by setting a new password using the OTP received via email. After successful reset, user can login with the new password.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Password recovery flow - Step 2
• After receiving reset OTP via email
• Setting new password

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates email + OTP combination
2. Validates new password meets requirements
3. Hashes and stores new password
4. Invalidates the OTP
5. Optionally invalidates existing sessions

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: Public

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• OTP expires in 10 minutes
• New password must meet security requirements
• Does NOT auto-login - user must login with new password

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show password strength indicator
• Confirm password with second field
• On success, redirect to login with success message

input.description: Email, OTP code, and new password

changePassword.description:
📋 CHANGE PASSWORD (WHILE LOGGED IN)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Allows authenticated users to change their password. Requires the current password for verification before setting the new password. This is for users who know their current password and want to update it.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Account settings/security page
• Regular password rotation
• User wants to strengthen password

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Verifies current password is correct
2. Validates new password meets requirements
3. Hashes and stores new password
4. Optionally invalidates other sessions

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER, SERVICE_PROVIDER

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Current password must be correct
• New password must meet security requirements
• Current session remains valid

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show password strength indicator
• Require password confirmation field
• Show success toast on completion

input.description: Current password for verification and new password

logout.description:
📋 USER LOGOUT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Logs out the current user by invalidating their refresh token. The access token will remain valid until it expires (15 minutes), but the refresh token can no longer be used to obtain new access tokens.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User clicks logout button
• Security logout (suspicious activity)
• Switching accounts

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Invalidates the refresh token
2. Clears server-side session data
3. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER, SERVICE_PROVIDER

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Access token valid until expiry (15 min)
• Refresh token immediately invalidated
• Clear tokens on client side after calling

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Clear all stored tokens (accessToken, refreshToken)
• Clear user state/context
• Redirect to login page
• Show 'Logged out successfully' message

updateProfile.description:
📋 UPDATE USER PROFILE

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Updates the current user's profile information. All fields are optional - only the provided fields will be updated. This is a partial update (PATCH-like behavior).

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Profile settings page
• Edit account information
• Update contact details

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates provided fields
2. Updates only specified fields
3. Returns updated user profile
4. Updates 'updatedAt' timestamp

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER, SERVICE_PROVIDER

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Email cannot be changed (use separate flow)
• All fields are optional
• Empty strings will update to empty

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Pre-fill form with current values from 'me' query
• Show loading state while updating
• Display success toast on completion

deleteAccount.description:
📋 DELETE OWN ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Permanently deletes the user's own account and all associated data. This action is IRREVERSIBLE. For SERVICE_PROVIDER accounts, this will also delete all their services.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Account deletion request from user
• GDPR/privacy compliance (right to be forgotten)
• User wants to leave platform

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Verifies user identity
2. Checks for active bookings (providers)
3. Deletes user account and all data
4. Removes associated services (for providers)
5. Invalidates all tokens

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER, SERVICE_PROVIDER

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• ACTION IS IRREVERSIBLE!
• Providers cannot delete if they have active bookings
• All user data is permanently removed
• Associated services are deleted

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show serious warning dialog
• Require password confirmation
• Require typing 'DELETE' to confirm
• Clear all local data after success

becomeProvider.description:
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
🏪 SERVICE PROVIDER MUTATIONS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
APIs for service providers to manage their business profile and service listings. Users must first upgrade to SERVICE_PROVIDER role before accessing these endpoints.
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📋 BECOME A SERVICE PROVIDER

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Upgrades a SERVICE_USER account to SERVICE_PROVIDER by creating a business profile. This is a one-way conversion - once a provider, the user gains access to service management features but retains all user capabilities.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• 'Become a Provider' onboarding flow
• User wants to start offering services
• Vendor registration process

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes user role from SERVICE_USER to SERVICE_PROVIDER
2. Creates ServiceProviderProfile with business details
3. Sets verification status to UNVERIFIED
4. Returns updated user with provider profile

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER only (existing providers get error)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Role change is permanent
• Provider starts as UNVERIFIED
• Must submit documents for verification
• Services won't be visible until VERIFIED
• Existing providers cannot call this

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Multi-step onboarding form
• Step 1: Business name & description
• Step 2: Location details (address, city, state)
• Step 3: Review & submit
• Explain verification process to user

input.description: Business profile information for the new provider

example.businessDescription: Professional plumbing for residential and commercial properties. 10+ years experience.

updateProviderProfile.description:
📋 UPDATE PROVIDER BUSINESS PROFILE

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Updates the business profile information for a SERVICE_PROVIDER. All fields are optional - only provided fields are updated. Use this to update business details after initial registration.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider settings page
• Update business information
• Change location/contact details
• Rebrand business name

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates provider has a profile
2. Updates only specified fields
3. Returns updated user with provider profile
4. Updates 'updatedAt' timestamp

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• All fields are optional
• Cannot change verification status
• Location changes may require re-verification

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Pre-fill form with current providerProfile data
• Show map for location updates
• Display current verification status

input.description: Business profile fields to update (all optional)

example.businessDescription: Award-winning plumbing services with 24/7 emergency support

createService.description:
📋 CREATE NEW SERVICE LISTING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Creates a new service listing for the provider. Services start in DRAFT status and must be submitted for admin approval before becoming visible to customers.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider adding new service
• Service creation form
• Expanding service offerings

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Creates service with DRAFT status
2. Associates service with provider profile
3. Links to specified category
4. Generates URL-friendly slug
5. Returns created service

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Service starts in DRAFT status (not visible)
• Provider must be VERIFIED for services to show (even if ACTIVE)
• Must call submitServiceForApproval after creation
• Category must exist and be active

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show category dropdown (from categories query)
• Allow multiple image uploads
• Price input with NGN currency
• Duration selector (in minutes)
• 'Save as Draft' and 'Submit for Review' buttons

example.name: Pipe Repair & Replacement
example.description: Professional pipe repair including diagnosis, repair, and pressure testing. All work guaranteed for 6 months.

updateService.description:
📋 UPDATE SERVICE LISTING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Updates an existing service listing. Provider can only update their own services. All fields are optional - only provided fields are updated.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Edit service details
• Update pricing
• Modify description
• Change images

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Verifies provider owns the service
2. Updates specified fields
3. Returns updated service
4. Updates 'updatedAt' timestamp

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own services only)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Can only update own services
• Cannot directly set status to ACTIVE
• Can set status to DRAFT or INACTIVE
• Major changes may require re-approval

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Pre-fill form with current service data
• Show current images with delete option
• Warn if changing approved service

example.description: Updated description with extended warranty info

deleteService.description:
📋 DELETE SERVICE LISTING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Permanently deletes a service listing. Provider can only delete their own services. This action cannot be undone.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Remove service no longer offered
• Clean up draft services
• Service discontinued

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Verifies provider owns the service
2. Checks for active bookings
3. Permanently deletes the service
4. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own services only)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Cannot delete services with active bookings
• Action is permanent and irreversible
• Associated images are also removed

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show confirmation dialog
• Warn about permanent deletion
• Disable for services with bookings

submitServiceForApproval.description:
📋 SUBMIT SERVICE FOR ADMIN APPROVAL

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Submits a DRAFT service for admin review. Changes the service status from DRAFT to PENDING_APPROVAL. Admin will review and either approve (ACTIVE) or reject (back to DRAFT with reason).

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider finished editing service
• Ready to make service visible
• After creating or updating service

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates service is in DRAFT status
2. Changes status to PENDING_APPROVAL
3. Adds to admin review queue
4. Returns updated service

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Bearer token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own services only)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Only DRAFT services can be submitted
• Admin reviews typically take 24-48 hours
• Provider will be notified of decision
• Rejected services can be edited and resubmitted

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show 'Submit for Review' button on drafts
• Explain review process and timeline
• Show pending status indicator
• Notify provider of approval/rejection

id.description: Service ID to submit for approval

adminLogin.description:
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
🔐 ADMIN MUTATIONS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
APIs for platform administrators to manage users, providers, services, and categories. Both ADMIN and SUPER_ADMIN roles can access these endpoints unless specifically noted.
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📋 ADMIN LOGIN / AUTHENTICATION

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Authenticates admin users with separate credentials from regular users. This endpoint is specifically for ADMIN and SUPER_ADMIN roles only. Regular SERVICE_USER or SERVICE_PROVIDER accounts will receive an authentication error.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin panel login page
• Admin dashboard authentication
• Separate from main user login

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates admin email/password
2. Verifies user has ADMIN or SUPER_ADMIN role
3. Updates lastLoginAt timestamp
4. Generates admin JWT tokens
5. Returns AdminAuthResponse (different from user AuthResponse)

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Completely separate from user login
• Regular users cannot use this endpoint
• Returns AdminAuthResponse, not AuthResponse
• Admins are created by SUPER_ADMIN only (no self-registration)

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Admin panel should be a separate app/route
• Store admin tokens separately from user tokens
• Check admin.role for permission-based UI
• Different UI for ADMIN vs SUPER_ADMIN

adminRefreshToken.description:
📋 REFRESH ADMIN ACCESS TOKEN

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Generates a new admin access token using a valid admin refresh token. Similar to user refreshToken but for admin sessions.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin access token expired
• Silent refresh in admin panel
• Maintaining admin session

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates admin refresh token
2. Verifies admin account is active
3. Generates new admin access token
4. Returns AdminRefreshTokenResponse

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required (uses refresh token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Admin refresh token expires in 30 days
• Must use admin token, not user token

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Implement token refresh interceptor for admin API calls

adminForgotPassword.description:
📋 ADMIN FORGOT PASSWORD

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Initiates password reset for admin accounts. Sends OTP to admin email.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin forgot password
• Admin account recovery

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Looks up admin by email
2. Generates reset OTP
3. Sends OTP via email

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN accounts

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Separate forgot password flow for admin panel

adminResetPassword.description:
📋 ADMIN RESET PASSWORD WITH OTP

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Completes admin password reset using OTP from email.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Complete admin password recovery
• After receiving admin reset OTP

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates admin email + OTP
2. Sets new admin password
3. Invalidates OTP

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Not required
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN accounts

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• OTP input + new password form
• Redirect to admin login on success

input.description: Admin email, OTP, and new password

adminChangePassword.description:
📋 ADMIN CHANGE PASSWORD

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Allows logged-in admins to change their password.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin account settings
• Security update
• Password rotation

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Verifies current admin password
2. Updates to new password

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Current password + new password form
• Password strength indicator

adminLogout.description:
📋 ADMIN LOGOUT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Logs out admin and invalidates admin refresh token.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin clicks logout
• End admin session

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Invalidates admin refresh token
2. Clears admin session data

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Clear admin tokens
• Redirect to admin login page

updateAdminProfile.description:
📋 UPDATE ADMIN PROFILE

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Updates the current admin's profile information.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin profile settings
• Update admin details

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Updates admin profile fields
2. Returns updated admin

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Pre-fill with current admin data from adminMe query

suspendUser.description:
📋 SUSPEND USER ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Suspends a user account, preventing them from logging in or performing any actions. Use for policy violations or security concerns. The user can be reactivated later.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User violated platform policies
• Reported for inappropriate behavior
• Security concern or fraud
• Temporary account restriction

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes user status to SUSPENDED
2. Invalidates user's active sessions
3. Records suspension reason
4. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• User cannot login while suspended
• All active tokens are invalidated
• Suspension reason is logged
• Can be reversed with activateUser

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Require reason input field
• Show in user management list
• Confirmation dialog before suspending

reason.example: Multiple policy violations reported by other users

activateUser.description:
📋 ACTIVATE/REACTIVATE USER ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Reactivates a suspended user account, restoring their ability to login and use the platform.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Restore user after suspension period
• User appeal approved
• Suspension was in error

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes user status to ACTIVE
2. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show 'Activate' button for suspended users
• Optional: Add note about reactivation reason

deleteUser.description:
📋 DELETE USER ACCOUNT (ADMIN ACTION)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Permanently deletes a user account and all associated data. This is an admin action for removing accounts - different from user self-deletion.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Remove spam/fake accounts
• User requested deletion (GDPR)
• Clean up test accounts

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Permanently deletes user account
2. Removes all associated data (provider profile, services)
3. Cannot be undone

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• ACTION IS PERMANENT AND IRREVERSIBLE
• Deletes all associated services for providers
• Cannot recover deleted data

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Require confirmation with user email
• Show warning about permanent deletion
• Log admin action for audit

approveProvider.description:
📋 APPROVE PROVIDER VERIFICATION

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Approves a provider's verification request, changing their status from PENDING to VERIFIED. Once verified, the provider's services can become visible to customers.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Review provider verification queue
• Provider documents are valid
• Approve legitimate business

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes provider verificationStatus to VERIFIED
2. Makes provider eligible to have visible services
3. Sends notification to provider
4. Returns updated user with provider profile

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Review documents before approving
• Verified providers' ACTIVE services become visible
• Provider is notified of approval

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show provider documents for review
• Display business details
• One-click approve button
• Optional: Add approval notes

rejectProvider.description:
📋 REJECT PROVIDER VERIFICATION

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Rejects a provider's verification request with a reason. Provider can address issues and resubmit for verification.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Documents are invalid/unclear
• Business doesn't meet criteria
• Suspicious application

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes verificationStatus to REJECTED
2. Records rejection reason
3. Sends notification with reason to provider
4. Returns updated user

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Always provide clear reason
• Provider can resubmit after fixing issues
• Services remain not visible

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Require rejection reason (mandatory)
• Predefined reasons + custom option
• Clear explanation for provider

reason.example: Business registration document is expired. Please upload a current document dated within the last 3 months.

approveService.description:
📋 APPROVE SERVICE LISTING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Approves a service that was submitted for review, changing status from PENDING_APPROVAL to ACTIVE. Service becomes visible to customers.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Review service moderation queue
• Service meets platform guidelines
• Content is appropriate

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes service status to ACTIVE
2. Service becomes visible (if provider is VERIFIED)
3. Notifies provider of approval
4. Returns updated service

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Review service content before approving
• Check pricing is reasonable
• Service only visible if provider is also VERIFIED

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show service details for review
• Display provider info
• Quick approve button

rejectService.description:
📋 REJECT SERVICE LISTING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Rejects a service listing, changing status back to DRAFT. Provider can edit and resubmit. Always provide a clear reason so provider knows what to fix.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Service violates guidelines
• Missing or incomplete information
• Inappropriate content
• Misleading description

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes service status to DRAFT
2. Records rejection reason
3. Notifies provider with reason
4. Returns updated service

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Provide clear, actionable reason
• Provider can edit and resubmit
• Service remains not visible

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Require rejection reason
• Predefined reasons + custom
• Clear guidance for provider

reason.example: Service description is too vague. Please provide more details about what is included and any terms/conditions.

suspendService.description:
📋 SUSPEND SERVICE LISTING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Suspends an active service, hiding it from customers. Use for policy violations or reported issues. Service can be reactivated.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Service reported for violations
• Misleading pricing/description
• Provider-related issues
• Content review required

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes service status to SUSPENDED
2. Service immediately hidden from customers
3. Records suspension reason
4. Notifies provider

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Immediately removes from public view
• Provider notified with reason
• Can be reactivated after review

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Require suspension reason
• Show in content moderation queue
• Option to reinstate

reason.example: Multiple customer complaints about misleading pricing. Service under review.

createCategory.description:
📋 CREATE SERVICE CATEGORY

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Creates a new category for organizing services. Categories help users browse and filter services.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Add new service type to platform
• Expand service offerings
• Platform growth

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Creates new category
2. Generates URL-friendly slug
3. Sets isActive to true by default
4. Returns created category

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Category name must be unique
• Slug auto-generated from name
• New categories are active by default

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Preview how category will appear
• Icon selector/uploader
• Description rich text editor

example.name: Plumbing
example.description: Professional plumbing services including repairs, installations, and maintenance for residential and commercial properties.

updateCategory.description:
📋 UPDATE CATEGORY

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Updates an existing category. Can modify details or activate/deactivate categories.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Edit category details
• Update description or icon
• Disable/enable category

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Updates specified fields
2. Returns updated category
3. If deactivated, hides associated services

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Deactivating hides associated services
• Reactivating shows them again

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Pre-fill form with current data
• Active/inactive toggle
• Show service count

deleteCategory.description:
📋 DELETE CATEGORY

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Permanently deletes a category. Will fail if the category has any associated services - services must be moved or deleted first.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Remove unused category
• Clean up test categories
• Category consolidation

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Checks for associated services
2. Deletes category if no services
3. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• CANNOT delete categories with services
• Move or delete services first
• Consider deactivating instead

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show service count before delete
• Block delete if services exist
• Suggest deactivation alternative

createAdmin.description:
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
👑 SUPER ADMIN MUTATIONS
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
APIs exclusively for SUPER_ADMIN users. These endpoints manage other admin accounts. Regular ADMIN users cannot access these endpoints.
════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📋 CREATE NEW ADMIN ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Creates a new admin account. Only SUPER_ADMIN can create other admins - there is no self-registration for admin accounts. New admins can be either ADMIN or SUPER_ADMIN role.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Onboard new admin team member
• Add platform moderator
• Create super admin

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Creates admin account with specified role
2. Admin can login immediately (no verification needed)
3. Sends credentials notification email
4. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Super Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SUPER_ADMIN only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Only SUPER_ADMIN can create admins
• New admin should change password on first login
• Choose role carefully (ADMIN vs SUPER_ADMIN)

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Role selector dropdown
• Generate temporary password option
• Show permissions for each role

suspendAdmin.description:
📋 SUSPEND ADMIN ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Suspends an admin account, preventing them from logging in. Use when an admin needs to be temporarily or permanently blocked.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Admin leaving company
• Security concern
• Policy violation
• Temporary access removal

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes admin status to SUSPENDED
2. Invalidates admin's tokens
3. Records suspension reason
4. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Super Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SUPER_ADMIN only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Cannot suspend yourself
• Admin immediately loses access
• Can be reactivated later

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Require reason input
• Confirmation dialog
• Cannot suspend current user

reason.example: Employee termination - access revoked per HR request

activateAdmin.description:
📋 ACTIVATE ADMIN ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Reactivates a suspended admin account, restoring their login access.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Restore admin access
• Temporary suspension ended
• Reinstate admin

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes admin status to ACTIVE
2. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Super Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SUPER_ADMIN only

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Show 'Activate' button for suspended admins

updateAdminRole.description:
📋 UPDATE ADMIN ROLE

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Changes an admin's role - either promote ADMIN to SUPER_ADMIN or demote SUPER_ADMIN to ADMIN.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Promote admin to super admin
• Demote super admin to admin
• Adjust admin privileges

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Changes admin role
2. Returns updated admin

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Super Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SUPER_ADMIN only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Cannot change your own role
• Be careful with SUPER_ADMIN promotions

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Role dropdown selector
• Show role permission differences
• Cannot modify self

deleteAdmin.description:
📋 DELETE ADMIN ACCOUNT

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Permanently deletes an admin account. This action cannot be undone.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Remove former employee admin
• Clean up test admin accounts
• Permanent removal required

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Permanently deletes admin account
2. Cannot be recovered
3. Returns success confirmation

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Super Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SUPER_ADMIN only

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Cannot delete yourself
• Action is permanent and irreversible
• Consider suspending instead

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Confirmation with admin email
• Warning about permanent deletion
• Cannot delete current user

createBooking.description:
📋 CREATE BOOKING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Creates a new service booking request. User selects a service, chooses date/time, provides address and notes.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User wants to book a service
• After browsing service details
• Schedule appointment with provider

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates service exists and is active
2. Checks scheduling constraints (min 2 hrs, max 30 days)
3. Creates booking with PENDING status
4. Calculates platform commission (10%)
5. Sends notification to provider

📤 RETURNS:
• Complete booking object with service and provider details
• Includes calculated pricing and commission

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (User token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER only

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Date/time picker (min 2 hours from now)
• Address autocomplete
• Optional notes textarea
• Show price breakdown before confirming

updateBooking.description:
📋 UPDATE BOOKING

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Updates booking details. Only PENDING bookings can be fully modified by users.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User wants to change booking date/time
• Update address or notes
• Modify before provider accepts

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists and belongs to user
2. Checks booking is in PENDING status
3. Updates allowed fields
4. Returns updated booking

📤 RETURNS:
• Updated booking object

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (User token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER (own bookings only)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Can only update PENDING bookings
• Cannot change service after creation

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Edit button only for PENDING bookings
• Pre-fill form with current values
• Show warning about modification limits

cancelBooking.description:
📋 CANCEL BOOKING (USER)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Allows user to cancel their own booking. Free cancellation if within 24 hours of acceptance.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• User changed their mind
• Can no longer make the appointment
• Need to reschedule (cancel and rebook)

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists and belongs to user
2. Checks cancellation eligibility
3. Changes status to CANCELLED
4. Records cancellation reason
5. Notifies provider

📤 RETURNS:
• Cancelled booking object

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (User token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_USER (own bookings only)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Free cancellation within 24 hours of provider acceptance
• Cannot cancel COMPLETED or already CANCELLED bookings

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Cancel button with confirmation dialog
• Reason input field
• Show cancellation policy

acceptBooking.description:
📋 ACCEPT BOOKING (PROVIDER)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Provider accepts a pending booking request, confirming they will perform the service.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider reviews booking and agrees to do it
• Provider confirms availability for the slot

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists and belongs to provider
2. Checks booking is in PENDING status
3. Changes status to ACCEPTED
4. Records acceptance timestamp
5. Notifies user of confirmation

📤 RETURNS:
• Accepted booking object

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Provider token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own service bookings only)

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Accept button on booking card
• Show booking details before accepting
• Update booking list after acceptance

rejectBooking.description:
📋 REJECT BOOKING (PROVIDER)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Provider rejects a pending booking request with a reason.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider is unavailable for the slot
• Service cannot be provided at the location
• Other scheduling conflicts

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists and belongs to provider
2. Checks booking is in PENDING status
3. Changes status to REJECTED
4. Records rejection reason
5. Notifies user with reason

📤 RETURNS:
• Rejected booking object

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Provider token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own service bookings only)

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Reason is required for user transparency
• Excessive rejections may affect provider rating

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Reject button with reason modal
• Predefined reason options + custom text
• Show rejection impact warning

startService.description:
📋 START SERVICE (PROVIDER)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Provider marks that they have started working on an accepted booking.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider arrives at location
• Provider begins the service work
• Mark job as in progress

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists and belongs to provider
2. Checks booking is in ACCEPTED status
3. Changes status to IN_PROGRESS
4. Records start timestamp
5. Notifies user that work has begun

📤 RETURNS:
• Updated booking object with IN_PROGRESS status

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Provider token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own service bookings only)

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• 'Start Service' button on accepted bookings
• Available on scheduled date
• Show timer after starting

completeService.description:
📋 COMPLETE SERVICE (PROVIDER)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Provider marks the service as completed after finishing the work.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Provider finishes the job
• Work is completed successfully
• Ready for user review and payment

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists and belongs to provider
2. Checks booking is in IN_PROGRESS status
3. Changes status to COMPLETED
4. Records completion timestamp
5. Triggers payment processing
6. Notifies user to leave review

📤 RETURNS:
• Completed booking object with final details

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Provider token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: SERVICE_PROVIDER (own service bookings only)

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• 'Mark Complete' button on in-progress bookings
• Confirmation dialog
• Show earnings summary after completion

adminCancelBooking.description:
📋 CANCEL BOOKING (ADMIN)

📝 DESCRIPTION:
Admin forcefully cancels any booking for dispute resolution or policy violations.

🎯 WHEN TO USE:
• Resolve user/provider disputes
• Policy violation detected
• Emergency cancellation needed
• Fraudulent booking detected

⚙️ WHAT IT DOES:
1. Validates booking exists
2. Changes status to CANCELLED
3. Records admin cancellation reason
4. Notifies both user and provider
5. May trigger refund process

📤 RETURNS:
• Cancelled booking object

🔐 AUTHENTICATION: Required (Admin token)
👥 ALLOWED ROLES: ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN

⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Can cancel bookings in any status
• Action is logged for audit trail
• Both parties are notified

💡 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION:
• Admin cancel button on any booking
• Reason is required
• Audit log display

CreateBookingInput.description: New booking request - serviceId (required), scheduledDate (required), scheduledTime (required), address (required), notes (optional)

UpdateBookingInput.description: Booking update fields - scheduledDate, scheduledTime, address, notes (all optional)

BookingFiltersInput.description: Booking search filters - status, startDate, endDate, serviceId, providerId, userId
